perturbation.original_text: The Participants engaged in testing the interoperability of their respective products (“Test Objects”) will have joint and several ownership of the results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the interoperability of the Test Objects (the “Test Results”), information concerning which will constitute Confidential Information of the Participants. Notwithstanding the foregoing paragraph, each Participant will be the owner of any results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the performance of its own Test Object, regardless of the source, (“Individual Results”), information concerning which will constitute Confidential Information of that Participant and may not be disclosed or used by the other Participant other than as permitted for Confidential Information received under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Participants engaged in testing the interoperability of their respective products (“Test Objects”) will have joint and several ownership of the results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the interoperability of the Test Objects (the “Test Results”), information concerning which will constitute Confidential Information of the Participants. Notwithstanding the foregoing paragraph, each Participant will be the owner of any results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the performance of its own Test Object, regardless of the source, (“Individual Results”), information concerning which will *not* constitute Confidential Information of that Participant and may not be disclosed or used by the other Participant other than as permitted for Confidential Information received under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will constitute' to 'will not constitute' for Individual Results, the clause now states that a participant's own performance data is NOT confidential. This directly contradicts the original statement which defines Individual Results as Confidential Information, and also undermines Section 5 related to disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Participants engaged in testing the interoperability of their respective products (“Test Objects”) will have joint and several ownership of the results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the interoperability of the Test Objects (the “Test Results”), information concerning which will constitute Confidential Information of the Participants. Notwithstanding the foregoing paragraph, each Participant will be the owner of any results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the performance of its own Test Object, regardless of the source, (“Individual Results”), information concerning which will *not* constitute Confidential Information of that Participant and may not be disclosed or used by the other Participant other than as permitted for Confidential Information received under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original statement to say that 'Individual Results' will *not* constitute Confidential Information. The Contradicted text (which is the same as the Changed Text in this case) also states that 'Individual Results' will *not* constitute Confidential Information. The Perturbation Explanation details that this change contradicts the original intention of keeping performance data confidential, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction because it undermines Section 5 related to disclosure of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Participant may take any and all actions under law against the Receiving Participant. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that the Open Mobil e Alliance is not responsible for any actions of its members, including, but not limited to, any breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Participant may take any and all actions under law against the Receiving Participant. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that the Open Mobil e Alliance *is* responsible for any actions of its members, including, but not limited to, any breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is not' to 'is' makes the Open Mobile Alliance responsible for its members' breaches. This contradicts the original clause stating they are not responsible, and introduces uncertainty about liability in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Participant may take any and all actions under law against the Receiving Participant. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that the Open Mobil e Alliance *is* responsible for any actions of its members, including, but not limited to, any breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Open Mobile Alliance is 'not' responsible for its members' actions, while the changed text asserts that the Open Mobile Alliance 'is' responsible for its members' actions. The 'Contradicted Text' contains the same language as the changed text, thus contradicting what was initially stated. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the agreement regarding liability, satisfying the definition of a structural flaw in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below until 18:00 GMT on December 31, 2021 or until supersede d by another non -disclosure agreement between the Open Mobile Alliance and Participant, provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall cease to apply to each item of Confidential Information five (5) years after such item of Confidential Information has been given, disclosed, required or supplied at or for the purposes of an Event.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below until 18:00 GMT on December 31, 2021 or until supersede d by another non -disclosure agreement between the Open Mobile Alliance and Participant, provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall *not* cease to apply to each item of Confidential Information five (5) years after such item of Confidential Information has been given, disclosed, required or supplied at or for the purposes of an Event.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall cease' to 'shall not cease', the confidentiality obligations are now indefinite, contradicting the original five-year limit. This creates uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality and could lead to disputes after the five-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below until 18:00 GMT on December 31, 2021 or until supersede d by another non -disclosure agreement between the Open Mobile Alliance and Participant, provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall *not* cease to apply to each item of Confidential Information five (5) years after such item of Confidential Information has been given, disclosed, required or supplied at or for the purposes of an Event.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by negating the five-year limit on confidentiality obligations. Where previously the obligations 'shall cease', now they 'shall not cease'. This directly contradicts the original clause by creating an indefinite period of confidentiality, breaking compatibility, making enforcement unclear, and fulfilling the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the Application Form constitute the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement. No modification to the Agreement will be binding, unless in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the Application Form constitute the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement. *Any* modification to the Agreement will be binding, *even if not* in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification makes ANY modification binding, even if not in writing or signed. This directly contradicts the original clause requiring written and signed modifications, creating ambiguity about how the agreement can be changed and enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the Application Form constitute the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement. *Any* modification to the Agreement will be binding, *even if not* in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that any modification to the agreement is binding, even if not in writing or signed. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, directly conflicts with the original text, which required modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as it is unclear whether a modification needs to be written and signed to be binding, thus creating ambiguity about how the agreement can be changed and enforced and adhering to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
